old_claim.note: Trade Guilds (Collegia) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Trade Routes better capture the distribution and export network that made terra sigillata a widely traded Roman tableware; collegia are not required by the current source-backed scope.

source_shape.source_locator: Roman red gloss pottery sections describe terra sigillata as industrial-scale Italian and Gaulish tableware that was widely exported and whose distribution helps reconstruct the ancient Roman economy.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Terra sigillata's source-backed scaling context is production and trade distribution, not formal collegia.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports workshops, industrial production, and export/distribution; it does not make formal craft associations a required prerequisite.

ontology_before: Terra Sigillata Pottery depended on Trade Guilds (Collegia), implying formal associations enabled the pottery type.
ontology_after: Terra Sigillata Pottery depends on Trade Routes for export and distribution, while pottery and early chemical arts cover the ceramic production lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: terra_sigillata_pottery now depends on trade_routes and no longer directly depends on trade_guilds_collegia.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to formal potters' collegia rather than red-slip tableware.
A stronger source shows Roman terra sigillata production required collegia as formal guild institutions.
A separate roman_pottery_collegia node is added and becomes the direct dependency.
short_reason: Trade distribution is sourced; formal collegia are not.
